name: Wind Force
description: An environmental air current modifies movement for characters, projectiles, particles, vehicles, or loose objects.
design_notes: Wind mechanics are readable when the affected layer mask, gust vector field, turbulence, and visual cues match the actual simulation. The mechanic should define whether wind is a constant region, pulsing hazard, weather effect, or traversal aid, and whether it affects only presentation or gameplay bodies.
design_purpose: Create environmental pressure that redirects movement, projectiles, gliding, or object placement.
Make weather and airflow matter in traversal, puzzles, survival, or vehicle handling.
Give designers a directional field that can be previewed visually and tuned per area.
player_fantasy: The player feels air currents pushing through the space and learns to ride, resist, or avoid them.

parameters.name: gust_vector_field_id
parameters.description: Data source defining direction, strength, and spatial falloff for the air current.

parameters.name: gust_acceleration_vector
parameters.description: Acceleration applied to affected gameplay bodies while inside the wind field.

parameters.name: turbulence_noise_scale
parameters.description: Noise multiplier used to vary gust direction or intensity without changing the authored main vector.

parameters.name: drag_coefficient
parameters.description: Resistance applied along or against the wind field for bodies with high surface area.

parameters.name: affected_layer_mask
parameters.description: Gameplay and cosmetic layers that should respond to the air current.

edge_cases: A projectile preview ignores wind while the fired projectile is affected by the gust field.
Wind pushes the player into a wall or hazard and collision correction amplifies the sideways motion.
Overlapping gust volumes produce abrupt direction changes because priority or blending is undefined.
Particles show a strong gust but gameplay bodies are excluded, creating misleading affordances.
common_bugs: Wind is applied every render frame instead of fixed step, changing displacement by frame rate.
Affected layer masks include heavy static props and wake too many sleeping bodies at once.
Turbulence noise is sampled nondeterministically and desyncs replay or multiplayer projectile paths.
Updrafts modify vertical velocity after jump and fall caps, bypassing gravity tuning rules.
balancing_notes: Strong gusts should be paired with visible wind direction, safe test spaces, or recovery routes.
Wind that affects projectiles can deepen aiming, but it should not make short-range combat feel random.
Pulsing gusts need rhythm and audio cues if players must time traversal through them.
accessibility_notes: Use readable particle direction, sound, and environment motion rather than relying only on subtle cloth or grass animation.
Offer reduced particle density or motion intensity while preserving gameplay-readable wind indicators.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent gusts as trigger volumes or vector-field assets that provide acceleration to eligible Rigidbody and character motors in FixedUpdate. Keep particle wind zones cosmetic unless they read the same gust_vector_field_id, and record deterministic noise seeds for replays.
implementation_notes.godot: Area2D or Area3D nodes can hold wind metadata and affect CharacterBody or RigidBody state during physics processing. Use groups or collision layers for affected_layer_mask, and debug draw arrows for field direction and falloff.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Physics Volumes, WindDirectionalSource for presentation, or custom components for gameplay acceleration. ProjectileMovement and CharacterMovement need explicit wind hooks, while replicated games should derive gust state from server-approved volume ids and deterministic pulses.
implementation_notes.web: In Phaser, Matter.js, or custom loops, query gust volumes during fixed integration and apply gust_acceleration_vector to selected bodies. Use seeded noise for turbulence and keep particle rendering subscribed to the same field data as gameplay.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses updrafts and wind-like traversal effects in exploration.

example_games.title: Journey
example_games.note: Uses wind and cloth-like motion as part of environmental traversal feel.
